meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no SaaS knowledge
content.seo_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress clients.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing your SaaS pricing. Leverage data-driven models by integrating Stripe APIs with automated usage tracking. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing infrastructure and Claude Code to implement dynamic pricing logic. This systematic approach ensures professional-grade revenue capture, even if you have zero prior SaaS architecture knowledge.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a new SaaS project is where most freelance dreams go to die. You have the code, but you lack the architecture to turn that code into a recurring paycheck. The anxiety of 'getting it wrong' keeps you coding features instead of building a business.

We are going to dismantle that barrier. By combining the rapid scaffolding of Antigravity with the logic-engine capabilities of Claude Code, we can build a pricing engine that scales as fast as your client's user base. It is time to stop being a coder and start being a SaaS architect.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the unit of value (e.g., storage, API calls, seats) to drive your pricing strategy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SaaS database schema that supports multi-tenant user roles, subscription status, and usage-based tracking metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a schema migration for a usage_logs table with columns: user_id, feature_type, and consumption_count'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Establish a secure bridge between your application and Stripe to handle payments and webhooks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate Stripe webhook handler in Node.js that listens for subscription.updated and invoice.payment_succeeded events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install stripe && touch stripe-webhook.js'

steps.title: Automate Usage-Based Logic
steps.description: Create the middleware that checks if a user has exceeded their current plan quota before executing a request.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks the user's current subscription tier against their daily API usage limit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze current middleware and inject a permission check for the /api/generate endpoint'

steps.title: Design the Pricing UI
steps.description: Build a responsive pricing table that dynamically updates based on the user's current plan and selected billing frequency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component for a three-tier pricing table with toggleable monthly/yearly billing states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a responsive PricingTable.tsx component using Tailwind CSS'

steps.title: Deploy and Test Webhooks
steps.description: Verify your infrastructure in a sandbox environment to ensure billing triggers are firing correctly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock test suite for the Stripe webhook integration to simulate subscription failures and upgrades.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run integration tests for the payment flow and confirm database updates'

pro_tips: Always start with a 'Free Tier' that uses a hard limit (e.g., 5 projects) rather than time-based trials to drive product adoption.
Use Stripe's 'Customer Portal' instead of building your own billing UI to save 20+ hours of development time.
Implement 'Overage Charges' early; it is easier to add them to your code base now than to retrofit them after you have 100+ users.
Separate your billing logic into a dedicated microservice or isolated folder to keep your core business logic clean and testable.
Always log every API call to Stripe; you will eventually need this data for customer support disputes and billing audits.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, focus on building an automated email sequence that triggers whenever a user hits 80% of their usage limit to encourage plan upgrades.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the architecture to launch a professional, revenue-ready SaaS product. Stop letting pricing complexity hold you back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
